content:
Spotify is truly the king of audio.

 

I admit. I’m a very slow reader.
It takes me ages to finish a book.

 

That’s why audiobooks work so well for me.
I loved when Spotify added audiobooks to Premium last year.

 

But not every book works well in audio.

 

Some books require more focus than just listening.
Sometimes it’s easier to go back to a paragraph, or a highlighted section in a physical copy. Other audiobooks are poorly narrated.

 

So I've found a system.
I listen to the audiobook and buy the physical book as well.
Most of the time, I only read a few sections on paper, but I complete the book through audio.

 

I've always wanted a way to start listening exactly from where I left off in the physical book. Sci-fi, I thought.

 

Well, Spotify just made it!

 

Now they're asking me to pay extra for Audiobook+. I think I'm gonna do it, they've earned it!

 

Jenni Romaniuk glad your book is included :)

images.alt: Mobile screen shows 'Building Distinctive Brand Assets' cover with a beta prompt about jumping between book and audiobook.

headline: Spotify adds Audiobook+ and lets you resume from the physical book
description: I explain using Spotify audiobooks with a physical copy and how Audiobook+ lets me resume where I left off, and I plan to subscribe.
